title: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes
hook: A neural network learns to invent new data by imagining hidden causes.
promise: You will grasp how variational autoencoders use the reparameterization trick to train deep generative models efficiently.
prerequisite_note: Bring your curiosity about how machines can imagine new examples from scratch.

glossary.plain: An invisible factor that explains patterns in the observed data.
A method for approximating complicated probability distributions with simpler ones.
A clever change of variables that lets you backpropagate through random sampling.
A neural network that maps data into a hidden representation.
A neural network that reconstructs data from the hidden representation.
A target objective for training that balances data reconstruction and regularization.

cards.title: How machines imagine new data
body: Discover how variational autoencoders learn to invent new examples by simulating hidden causes.

cards.title: What if a neural net could dream?
body: Imagine a machine that looks at thousands of handwritten digits and learns not just to copy them, but to invent new ones that look real.
To do this, the machine needs to guess the invisible factors that shape real data, then use those guesses to generate entirely fresh samples.

cards.title: From data to imagination
body: First, the model sees real data and tries to guess the hidden variables that could have produced it.
Next, it learns to use those hidden guesses to reconstruct the original data, adjusting itself to get better at both guessing and recreating.
Finally, it can invent new data by sampling from the hidden space and decoding those samples into new outputs.
cards.caption: The VAE learns to reverse engineer and then simulate the data-making process.

cards.title: How did we train generative models before?
body: Toggle to compare the old approach of sampling and optimization with the VAE's reparameterization trick.
cards.offNote: Gradients are noisy or impossible to compute, making training slow or unstable.
cards.onNote: Gradients flow cleanly through the network, enabling fast and stable end-to-end learning.

cards.title: The reparameterization trick unlocks deep generative learning
body: Training a model to guess hidden causes usually means dealing with randomness, which breaks the chain of gradients needed for learning.
The reparameterization trick cleverly rewrites the random step so that gradients can pass through, making neural generative models trainable end to end.

cards.title: How much do we care about realism versus variety?
body: Adjust the slider to see how the VAE balances reconstructing exact data versus exploring new possibilities.
cards.low: The model memorizes data, but struggles to invent anything new.
cards.mid: The model balances copying the data and generating plausible new examples.
cards.high: The model explores wild variations, but outputs may stray from reality.

cards.title: Pick the VAE's key ingredients
body: Choose the parts needed to make a variational autoencoder work.

choices.label: Encoder network
choices.note: Maps data to a distribution over hidden variables.

choices.label: Latent variable
choices.note: Represents the hidden cause behind each data point.

choices.label: Reparameterization trick
choices.note: Allows gradients to flow through sampling.

choices.label: Decoder network
choices.note: Reconstructs data from the hidden variables.

choices.label: ELBO objective
choices.note: Guides learning by balancing reconstruction and regularization.

choices.label: Stochastic optimization
choices.note: Handles large datasets efficiently during training.

cards.title: What changed after VAEs?
body: Before VAEs, training deep generative models with continuous latent variables was slow or impractical.
The VAE made it possible to learn powerful generative models efficiently, inspiring many advances in unsupervised learning and creative AI.
cards.caption: VAEs brought generative modeling into the deep learning era by making it efficient and scalable.

cards.title: Why VAEs are foundational
body: The VAE introduced a practical way to train neural networks that can imagine and create new data, not just classify or predict.
Its core trick, the reparameterization, became a building block for almost every modern deep generative model.

cards.title: How a VAE works in four steps

recap.label: Encode
recap.note: Map data into a hidden variable distribution.

recap.label: Sample
recap.note: Draw a hidden cause using the reparameterization trick.

recap.label: Decode
recap.note: Transform the hidden variable back into data.

recap.label: Optimize
recap.note: Adjust everything to balance realism and variety.

cards.title: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes
body: Read the original paper for full mathematical details and experimental results.
